top_insights: The power of storytelling and narrative control in business and life
Self-discipline, routine creation, and overcoming distraction as keys to growth
Validation of success through tangible financial results and status signals
Contrarian takes on popular topics (e.g., AI hype, competition myths, historical figures)
Creator-led entrepreneurship solving real-world problems with tangible impact
hook_patterns: Insight-driven hooks presenting a specific, often surprising fact or observation
Contrarian statements challenging common beliefs or popular narratives
Direct, punchy declarations of personal achievement or philosophical truths
emotional_drivers: Validation (seeking confirmation of status, success, or worldview)
Ambition (desire for growth, leverage, and financial freedom)
Curiosity (interest in behind-the-scenes truths or historical revelations)
emerging_narrative: There is a strong resonance with 'real-world' impact over abstract theory. Audiences are connecting with stories of tangible results (money, products built, habits formed) and are increasingly skeptical of hype (AI, competition), preferring grounded, contrarian insights that validate their own drive for autonomy and success.

viral_examples.author: dickiebush
viral_examples.text: “Cha-ching” $5,000 hit my PayPal account. “This has to be a typo...” I thought to myself. So I messaged him. “Hey, did you mean to send $5,000?” His response: “Yeah, is that enough?” And just like that, Ghostwriting completely changed my life trajectory. Here’s the story

viral_examples.author: dickiebush
viral_examples.text: Steve Jobs once said: "The most powerful person is the storyteller." And luckily, storytelling is a skill. But most people have never tried to learn – because no one showed them where to start. So here are 6 of the best storytelling threads (so you can start learning today):

viral_examples.author: dickiebush
viral_examples.text: The single most powerful habit for personal growth: Journaling. Over the past 5 years, I've journaled every single morning—and along the way, I've: •Answered over 1,000 questions •Tested every app, pen, & notebook But I always return to a pen, paper, and these 5 prompts:

viral_examples.author: paulg
viral_examples.text: Every picture of JP Morgan published in his lifetime (and thus nearly every picture published since) was heavily retouched. This is what he actually looked like.

viral_examples.author: dickiebush
viral_examples.text: The most creative company of the last 30 years: Pixar. Back in 2011, Pixar storyboard artist Emma Coats shared their "22 Rules For Storytelling." And the rules are a must-read for writers, entrepreneurs, and anyone who wants to tell captivating stories. Here's the breakdown:

winning_patterns: Insight-driven hooks presenting a specific, often surprising fact or observation
Contrarian statements challenging common beliefs or popular narratives
Direct, punchy declarations of personal achievement or philosophical truths
